action.reasoning: Observation: Persistent 'Image features and image tokens do not match' (e.g. 416 vs 416) even with matching debug counts; both train.py and predict.py remain truncated/incomplete in workspace (incomplete loop/optimizer/save/metrics in train.py; incomplete point coord handling + no JSON dump in predict.py). test_prep.py works and confirms data has bbox/point targets. Analysis: Root cause is dual processor calls (prompt vs full) leading to subtle template/placeholder differences in Qwen2VL (different pad counts in model's internal get_placeholder_mask despite printed match), combined with abnormal pixel_values shape [1664,1176] (flattened patches) and incomplete training code preventing any forward pass success after 13 iters. Single full-conversation processor call + prompt_length via separate tokenizer(prompt_text) + unsqueeze for bs=1 + correct tensor prep will align features/tokens. Complete non-truncated scripts with robust Dataset, manual bs=1 loop (64 samples/32 steps for ~30s iter), proper label masking, AdamW, metrics.jsonl, full save (adapter+processor+grid). Complete predict.py with all parsers, rescaling (grid=1000), clipping, defaults, JSON output per contract. This follows standard Qwen2VL SFT pattern. Decision: Overwrite train.py and predict.py with FULL working code, run test_prep+train (small scale for speed), then submit_eval to finally obtain first grounding_score + accurate time measurement before scaling data/epochs/LR/targets.
